cases.focus: GET /wp/v2/settings route matching, permission callback, callback execution, response wrapping, and matched route metadata
POST dispatch validates route args, sanitizes request params, updates options, and returns the refreshed settings response
invalid editable request parameter becomes a rest_invalid_param response before controller update logic runs
false permission callback becomes rest_forbidden with the logged-out authorization status code
false permission callback becomes rest_forbidden with the logged-in authorization status code
unknown REST route is converted to a 404 rest_no_route response
rest_pre_dispatch can short-circuit dispatch and is normalized to a WP_REST_Response
rest_request_before_callbacks can return WP_Error and skip endpoint callback execution
rest_dispatch_request can replace the endpoint callback result while preserving response wrapping and matched metadata

native_boundaries.reason: The fixture uses deterministic current_user_can() and is_user_logged_in() boundaries to isolate REST dispatch permission behavior. Full users/roles/capabilities remain later domain work.
The endpoint runs through real option functions, but a deterministic wpdb/cache test double supplies rows instead of a live database. Live database storage parity is covered by WPHX-305.
This fixture uses internal dispatch. HTTP serving, headers, CORS, JSON encode/decode output, and web-server integration remain separate REST/E2E work.
rest_pre_dispatch, rest_request_before_callbacks, and rest_dispatch_request are intentionally PHP-native plugin extension boundaries.
Route registration, route matching table shape, PHP callbacks, and request/response object ABI remain native WordPress PHP surfaces in this linked candidate.

remaining_gaps.detail: WP_REST_Server::serve_request(), headers, CORS, body parsing, JSON response emission, and web-server integration are outside this internal dispatch candidate.
The candidate is installed into a mirrored source tree. A packaged distribution test with upstream class-wp-rest-server.php replacement and no fallback remains future work.
Capability and logged-in state are deterministic boundaries here. Full user/role/capability parity remains outside this endpoint dispatch slice.
This fixture covers selected REST settings dispatch traces. Full upstream REST PHPUnit ratcheting remains part of PHP first-party closure.
